05b Copying the ScalableOS into your Notion Dashboard - YouTube.mp4
Howdy y'all! So just gonna show you how to make a copy of the Notion dashboard so that you have it in of your Notion account. Again, you will have the link here. It will also be shared with you inside of the resources area in the members area. So you will have that. You'll also have this walkthrough here and the link here. So again, go ahead and copy this link. I'm going to open up Safari so that I can show you what it looks like. Without having a dashboard existing, so you go ahead and copy that link in there. It'll show up. Once you open it, it'll look something like this. On the upper right-hand side here on this menu, you'll see a button that says 'duplicate.' Go ahead and click that.
It'll bring up this here menu. You'll have the option to choose which workspace to duplicate the template into. So go ahead and choose the one for your business. Click that, it'll start. You know, your computer will start thinking, its brain will go to work copying over the template. And then again, you'll have this here. And now you will have the template inside of your own Notion. Again, you'll see over here that you have the dashboard template. You can move it. You can move it into your workspace. So that way, right now, you have it here. It'll be shared with any of your team members that you have on there. And again, that's just to. make a copy inside of your own dashboard. And again, I'll send along the step-by-step instructions in text as well. And if you have any trouble with that, again, please feel free to email me. It's Kyle, K Y L E dot B O W E at scalable . co. Or you can ask questions inside of the members area. And again, we'll send you the step-by-step instructions of this as well.
